DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT LINDSAY LEE'SM OTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS - THIS MOTION TO COMPEL WAS ORIGINALLY NOTICED FOR A HEARING ON FEBRUARY 18, 2016. IN THE MOVING PAPERS, DEFENDANT COMPLAINED THAT, MORE THAN THREE MONTHS AFTER SERVICE OF THE REQUESTS, PLAINTIFF HAD FAILED TO PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS ON THE PRETEXT THAT A PROTECTIVE ORDER WAS NEEDED. THE DELAY BENEFITTED PLAINTIFF, BECAUSE DEFENDANT UNDERSTANDABLY DEFERRED TAKING THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF'S PRINCIPAL UNTIL IT HAD RECEIVED DOCUMENTS IN DISCOVERY. SOME FIVE DAYS AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED, A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER WAS COMPLETED. EXH. 22 TO DECL. IN OPPOS. SINCE THEN, PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED SOME 3,000 DOCUMENTS COMPRISING 16,000 PAGES, REPORTED THAT ITS PRODUCTION IS SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLETE, AND INVITED DEFENDANT TO MEET AND CONFER OVER ANY PERCEIVED DEFICIENCIES. IN ITS REPLY ON THIS MOTION, DEFENDANT SAYS: "THERE ARE SEVERAL CLASSES OF DOCUMENTS THAT I BELIEVE ARE MISSING." LEE DECL. AT PARA. 4. GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF REPORTS THAT IT COLLECTED 250 GIGABYTES OF ELECTRONICALLY STORED INFORMATION, AND PRODUCED BUT 16,000 PAGES - OR CONSERVATVELY LESS THAN ONE-HALF GIGABYTE OF DATA PER INDUSTRY STANDARDS -- THERE MAY BE REASON FOR DOUBT. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR VIA VIDEO CONFERENCE NO LATER THAN APRIL 20, 2016 TO RESOLVE THE QUESTION OF ANY PERCEIVED DEFICIENCIES IN PLAINTIFF'S PRODUCTION, AND PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SERVE VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES, AND MAKE ANY ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE TO DEFENDANT, NO LATER THAN APRIL 29. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS OR COSTS ON THIS MOTION ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM; CHARLES RAGAN CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stuart Gasner of Keker & Van Nest LLP telephone number 415-676-2209 for Palintiff JHB LLC.
Briggs Matheson of Keker & Van Nest LLP telephone number 415-773-6616 for Cross-Defendants JHB LLC, et al.
Eric Bjorgum of Karish & Bjorgum PC telephone number 213-785-8070 for and with Defendant Lindsay Lee.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS JOHN BURBANK, PASSPORT CAPITAL, LLC, PASSPORT VENTURES, LLC, PASSPORT CAPITOL VENTURES, LLC, JHB VENTURES LLC, JHB VENTURES SECURITY HOLDINGS LLC AND NIMBLE VENTURES LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is:
a) Overruled as to the second cause of action for fraud, the third cause of action for breach of contract, and the fifth cause of action for promissory estoppel; b) sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for conversion, the tenth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing and the eleventh cause of action for deceit; and c) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for interference with prospective economic advantage. The second cause of action sufficiently pleads all of the elements of a claim for false promise. (CACI 1902). The third cause of action sufficiently alleges all of the elements of a breach of an oral contract claim. The fourth cause of action for conversion fails because that claim is not based on anything more than alleged breaches of contract. The fifth cause of action sufficiently alleges clear and unambiguous promises as well as the other elements of a promissory estoppel claim, which may be alleged in the alternative to a claim for breach of contract. The sixth cause of action does not allege independently wrongful conduct. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Lee is given the opportunity to allege that his prospective advantages with the companies he invested in were interfered with by cross-defendants by conduct that is wrongful apart from the interference itself. The tenth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant fails because there is not allegation of any breach of any terms other than alleged express terms of the contract. The eleventh cause of action for deceit fails because it is an impermissible amalgam of all four recongized forms of fraud (intentional misrepresentation, false promise, intentional concealment and negligent misrepresentation). To the extent that the eleventh cause of action is based on intentional or negligent misrepresentations, it fails because it does not allege representations as to past facts. To the extent that it is based on intentional concealment, it fails because it does not allege the concealment of any facts that cross-defendants had a duty to disclose. To the extent that it is based on false promise, it is duplicative of the second cause of action. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Lindsay Lee To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
ATTY. BRIGGS J. MATHESON OF KEKER & VAN NEST LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-773-6616 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LINDSAY LEE'sM otion To Compel Production Of Documents - This motion to compel was originally noticed for a hearing on February 18, 2016. In the moving papers, defendant complained that, more than three months after service of the requests, plaintiff had failed to produce any documents on the pretext that a protective order was needed. The delay benefitted plaintiff, because defendant understandably deferred taking the deposition of plaintiff's principal until it had received documents in discovery. Some five days after the motion was filed, a stipulated protective order was completed. Exh. 22 to Decl. in Oppos. Since then, plaintiff has produced some 3,000 documents comprising 16,000 pages, reported that its production is substantially complete, and invited defendant to meet and confer over any perceived deficiencies. In its Reply on this motion, defendant says: "There are several classes of documents that I believe are missing." Lee Decl. at para. 4. Given that plaintiff reports that it collected 250 gigabytes of electronically stored information, and produced but 16,000 pages - or conservatvely less than one-half gigabyte of data per industry standards -- there may be reason for doubt. The parties are directed to meet and confer in person or via video conference no later than April 20, 2016 to resolve the question of any perceived deficiencies in plaintiff's production, and plaintiff is directed to serve verified supplemental responses, and make any additional documents available to defendant, no later than April 29. All requests for sanctions or costs on this motion are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem; Charles Ragan Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt
